objective: Deliver a runnable Python vertical slice for bounded queues, monotonic deadlines, cooperative cancellation and bounded in-memory idempotency, with a local CLI demo and redacted JSONL error records.
non_goals: Do not implement durable journal, outbox, inbox, ACK or replay persistence; those belong to M01-S3.
Do not implement WebSocket/control-plane transport, service supervision, model inference, speech, memory, avatar, game or livestream behavior.
Do not add external runtime dependencies or run deep/repeat/soak verification in fast-development mode.
contracts: Queue capacity is a hard upper bound and overflow behavior is explicit.
Deadlines use monotonic time and fail with a stable error code.
Cancellation is cooperative, idempotent and carries a redacted reason.
Concurrent operations with one idempotency key execute the side effect once; completed results replay until TTL expiry.
Every demo failure record is local JSONL with stable error code and correlation ID, with secret-like fields redacted.
acceptance_criteria: Reject, drop-oldest and wait overflow policies never exceed configured queue capacity.
Deadline and cancellation interrupt waits without leaking pending asyncio tasks.
Concurrent and replayed idempotent calls execute the side effect exactly once while registry memory remains bounded.
The owner can run one PowerShell demo command and see queue, timeout, cancellation and deduplication results as JSON.
Expected demo failures create redacted JSONL records under var/logs without writing secrets.
The focused unit suite completes once on the owner machine in fast-development mode.
